text.title: Agency Rebrand Methodology Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Agency Rebrand Methodology application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Notify active clients ≥30 days before public rebrand via personal email from founder. Include: new name, why, what changes (contract addendum), what does not (team, work, contacts).
rule.rationale: Surprise rebrands trigger churn cycles; 30-day notice converts uncertainty into routine admin.

rule.statement: Issue addenda assigning existing contracts to the new entity. Do NOT force renewal. Renewals expose every term to renegotiation.
rule.rationale: Standard M&A practice: assign, don't re-paper, to preserve commercial terms.

rule.statement: Build a 1:1 redirect map for every URL with traffic > 0. Deploy 301s the day the new domain goes live; keep ≥24 months.
rule.rationale: Without 301s, 60-80% of accrued SEO equity is lost in 90 days (Moz / Ahrefs case studies).

rule.statement: Migrate top 10 case studies to new domain BEFORE launch. New site goes live with a working portfolio, not 'coming soon'.
rule.rationale: Empty portfolio at launch resets credibility to zero (first-impression weight).

rule.statement: 30 days post-launch, audit redirect coverage, organic traffic, churn vs baseline, brand mentions. Surface deltas explicitly.
rule.rationale: Most rebrand failures show up at day 30-60; explicit audit catches them in the recoverable window.
